THIS TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN LIMESTONE COUNTY, ALABAMA AT 404 PM CST NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF PERSIMMON TREE ROAD AND SINIARD ROAD, WHERE A FEW TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. IT MOVED TO THE NORTHEAST, SNAPPING AND UPROOTING NUMEROUS TREES AS IT CROSSED TOMMY HILL ROAD AND LESTER ROAD. AS THE TORNADO CROSSED HOLLY LANE, IT DOWNED DOZENS OF TREES. IT CONTINUED ITS NORTHEASTWARD PROGRESSION, UPROOTING TREES ALONG SALEM MINOR HILL ROAD AND GARDNER HOLLY ROAD. IT THEN MOVED INTO GILES COUNTY, TENNESSEE AROUND 412 PM CST. IN TENNESSEE, THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST WHERE NASA SATELLITE IMAGERY SHOWED IT BLEW DOWN DOZENS OF TREES SOUTH OF HAGAN ROAD. ON THE EASTERN END OF HAGAN ROAD, TWO HOMES RECEIVED MINOR DAMAGE AND SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED. ANOTHER HOME HAD MINOR DAMAGE ON CASE ROAD AT BETHEL ROAD. THE WORST DAMAGE OCCURRED ON ALF HARRIS ROAD WHERE SEVERAL HOMES AND OUTBUILDINGS RECEIVED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE. THE TORNADO THEN TURNED MORE EASTWARD AND PASSED JUST NORTH OF THE PROSPECT COMMUNITY, BEFORE MORE HOMES AND OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED ON LIBERTY ROAD, KELLEY ROAD, AND PROSPECT ELKTON ROAD. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST WEST OF ELKTON, TENNESSEE. SPECIAL THANKS TO LIMESTONE COUNTY EMA AND GILES COUNTY EMA FOR THEIR HELP WITH THIS STORM DAMAGE SURVEY.

MARSHALL COUNTY EMA REPORTED A 3 MILE LONG BY 1/2 MILE WIDE SWATH OF WIND DAMAGE OCCURRED ACROSS THE NORTHEAST SIDE OF LEWISBURG. A MOBILE HOME ON VERONA ROAD LOST MUCH OF ITS ROOF AND SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN WITH ONE FALLING ONTO A HOUSE. ALONG NASHVILLE HIGHWAY FROM ELLINGTON PARKWAY TO OLD FARMINGTON ROAD, SEVERAL BUSINESSES SUFFERED MINOR ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE AND A POLE BARN COLLAPSED. FURTHER TO THE NORTHEAST, SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND A BARN WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED ON HOLLY GROVE ROAD AND LONG DISTANCE ROAD. THE MARSHALL COUNTY EOC JUST SOUTHWEST OF WHERE THE DAMAGE BEGAN MEASURED A PEAK WIND GUST OF 54 MPH.

FACEBOOK REPORTS AND PHOTOS SHOW SIGNIFICANT WIND DAMAGE AROUND THE AREA OF HIGHWAY 70 AND HOLLIS CREEK ROAD. BEANS DIESEL PERFORMANCE SHOP HAD PART OF THE ROOF BLOWN OFF, AND A STORAGE BARN WAS DESTROYED AT A LUMBER YARD ACROSS THE HIGHWAY. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 3 WSW WOODBURY. DRONE VIDEO FOOTAGE, SOCIAL MEDIA REPORTS AND PHOTOS INDICATE A 1/2 MILE LONG BY 1/2 MILE WIDE MICROBURST STRUCK THE AREA AROUND HIGHWAY 70 AT HOLLIS CREEK ROAD. BEANS DIESEL PERFORMANCE SHOP HAD PART OF THE ROOF BLOWN OFF, AND A STORAGE BARN WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED AT A LUMBER YARD ACROSS THE HIGHWAY. DEBRIS FROM THE STORAGE BARN WAS BLOWN UP TO 300 YARDS AWAY. TWO OUTBUILDINGS WERE ALSO DESTROYED ON HALEY LANE AND HOLLIS CREEK ROAD, AND AROUND TWO DOZEN TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED IN THE AREA. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED UP TO 80 MPH. SPECIAL THANKS TO CANNONSEVEREWX FOR ASSISTANCE WITH THIS STORM SURVEY.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM MANCHESTER. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ACROSS MANCHESTER INCLUDING AT DAVE KING PARK, A TRAILER COURT ON OAKDALE STREET, WOODLAND STREET, SOUTH SPRING STREET, AND OTHER AREAS.
LARGE SECTION OF THE RAIDER ACADEMY GYM WAS BLOWN OFF. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED UP TO 75 MPH.

SIGNIFICANT WIND DAMAGE OCCURRED ACROSS MANCHESTER. TWO GAS STATIONS AT INTERSTATE DR AND HWY 55 SUFFERED AWNING AND ROOF DAMAGE, A NEARBY BUILDING LOST MUCH OF ITS ROOF AND WALLS, AND SEVERAL SIGNS WERE BLOWN DOWN IN THE AREA. RAIDER ACADEMY ON HWY 55 SUFFERED ROOF DAMAGE TO THE GYM. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN INCLUDING AT DAVE KING PARK, A TRAILER COURT ON OAKDALE STREET, WOODLAND STREET, SOUTH SPRING STREET, AND OTHER AREAS.

A SMALL BUT INTENSE 1/2 MILE LONG BY 250 YARDS WIDE MICROBURST STRUCK AN AREA ALONG INTERSTATE DRIVE FROM AROUND MONOGARD DRIVE SOUTHEASTWARD TO HWY 55. SHEET METAL WAS BLOWN OFF A BUILDING INTO A STREET SIGN ON MONOGARD DRIVE. LARGE SECTIONS OF THE SHEET METAL ROOF WERE ALSO BLOWN OFF THE FARMERS COOP BUILDING ON THE WEST SIDE OF INTERSTATE DRIVE, AND A BP GAS STATION CANOPY LOST METAL PANELS. ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE ROADWAY, ONE HOME LOST A LARGE SECTION OF ITS ROOF AND A FRONT PORCH COLUMN, AND SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED IN THE YARD. ANOTHER GAS STATION ALSO SUFFERED ROOF DAMAGE, AND A NEARBY WAREHOUSE BUILDING LOST PART OF ITS ROOF AND HAD A CINDER BLOCK WALL COLLAPSE. DAMAGE WAS BLOWN FROM NW TO SE. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED UP TO 85 MPH.
